title: Kendle
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Kendle.jpg]] |name=Kendle |homeworld=[[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]][[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Iskalon[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] |species=[[Inleshat/Legends|Inleshat]]{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |feathers= |eyes=Red |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[The School]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I am the mate of Mone, son of Primor! All of the School swam in the ways and directions of Primor's choosing... till the bombs killed him! Until the Empire releases Mone, I will speak for the School... and I say that these three are responsible, and must pay...|Kendle|}} '''Kendle''' was an [[Inleshat/Legends|Inleshat]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who briefly took over as leader of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] on [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. The wife of [[Mone]] and daughter-in-law of [[Primor (Inleshat)|Primor]], the School's leader, Kendle became an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] informant after the Empire's brutal subjugation of the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]]. Kendle's correspondence with Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]] led to the arrest of several [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents on [[Gamandar]], and after Tower devastated Iskalon by triggering a massive [[Iskalon Effect|tidal wave]]—killing Primor—Kendle took control of the School and incited them to attack Rebels [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. This arrangement only lasted a short time, as Kendle was [[Death/Legends|killed]] shortly after by a [[chiaki]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Oh, Luke… I feel so… responsible…''"
"''No! No one is responsible! No one but her!|Leia Organa and Kiro|[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]]}} Kendle was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], and she eventually fell in love with and married [[Mone]], a fellow [[Iskalonian]] and the son of [[Primor (Inleshat)|Primor]], the leader of the School. Kendle cared deeply for her husband and father-in-law and resolved to keep them safe no matter the cost. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] came to the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]] and began to subjugate the neighboring worlds of [[Gamandar]] and [[Telfrey]], Kendle feared for the fate of her world and decided to aid the Empire in order to protect her family from the same type of destruction. When the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] heroes [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] arrived on Iskalon to open negotiations with the School, Kendle subsequently opened communication with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Griggor Tower]], the head of the Imperial operation on Gamandar.[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] Kendle told Tower of the presence of the Rebels, and then manipulated the Rebels by dropping hints that what they sought was located on Gamandar. Calrissian and Chewbacca decided to follow up on the tip, but they also brought Kendle's husband, Mone with them. After watching the Rebels depart, Kendle contacted Tower again and told him about the two remaining Rebels that had decided to explore the underwater city of [[Pavillion]]. Unfortunately for Kendle, during this communication, another Iskalonian, [[Kiro]] overheard her selling the Rebels out to the Empire. Kiro was a firm supporter of rebellion against the Empire after they had slaughtered a delegation of Iskalonians who had tried to sue for peace. Kiro grappled with Kendle, trying to kill the traitor, but the attempt ended up smashing both of their [[Water tank|water helmets]] which allowed them to enter Pavillion. Kiro was forced to abandon his attempt so that he could rush back to the ocean, but Kendle was badly hurt and unable to move. As Kendle began to suffocate without her water helmet, Skywalker, Organa, and Primor happened to spot Kendle's plight. Skywalker and Organa rushed to enter the city and aid Kendle while Primor watched anxiously. The two Rebels reached the struggling Iskalonian, but it seemed that they would be too late to save her. However, unbeknownst to the group, Kendle's leaked information to Admiral Tower had caused him to make a rash decision. An interplanetary missile had been fired at the city of Pavillion in hopes of killing Organa and Skywalker. The massive explosion of the missile caused an enormous [[Iskalon Effect|tidal wave]] which smashed against Pavillion and destroyed the underwater city. Luckily for Kendle, Pavillion's destruction let in an inrush of water, and she was saved from suffocating. After coming back to her senses, Kendle saw the two Rebels struggling to escape the wreckage of the city to reach the surface. Angry that the Imperials had gone to such measures to see them both destroyed, Kendle tried to lead them astray in an attempt to drown them. However, Kiro once again appeared and tried to execute the traitor. Kendle was forced to flee the scene, allowing the two Rebels to eventually reach the surface. Back on the surface, Kendle witnessed the carnage that had come about because of her actions. Many Iskalonians had died in the attack, including her beloved father-in-law. Kendle tried to pay her respects by grieving over Primor's lifeless body, but Kiro once again appeared in close pursuit. Kendle ran from the scene, but soon returned with members of the School in another attempt to destroy the two Rebels. Kendle declared herself as the new leader of the School following Primor's death and accused the Rebels of bringing this terrible catastrophe to her world. The School began to attack the Rebels and Kiro, but the three were able to fight off the assault. However, the School continued to push and it seemed that Kendle would soon be victorious. Unfortunately for Kendle's plans, just as her group moved in for the kill, a wild [[chiaki]] arose from the depths. As everyone scattered from the area, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] arrived and slew the creature. As the monstrous serpent gave its last breath, it began to topple onto the battleground, and its dead body smashed directly on top of Kendle, killing her instantly. The ''Millennium Falcon'' landed immediately afterward, and Mone left the vessel, distraught over his wife's death and betrayal. Mone declared himself the new leader of the School, and bitter over the loss of his family, he declared that the School would no longer deal with air breathers due to the pain that they had caused. The Rebels were forced to leave Iskalon, and the School departed the wreckage of Pavillion. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Kill them! Kill the aliens, and that evil little traitor! They are to blame for this carnage... and for the death of Primor... and for the loss of my Mone!|Kendle|}} Kendle was devoted to her husband Mone, and was able to evade suspicion and earn the trust of the Rebels upon their visit to Iskalon. But far from her image as merely Mone's loving wife, Kendle was actually a canny and conniving operator, fooling the Rebel visitors to Iskalon by working as an Imperial informant right under their noses and briefly seizing control of the School in the chaos following the tidal wave. During her short period commanding the School, Kendle revealed a burning wrath towards those she felt threatened her family and way of life, using the School's collective will as a weapon before her death. ==Behind the scenes== Kendle appeared in three issues of the original [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comic series in [[1983]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 74}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 75}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 76}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kendle }} [[Category:Agents of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Inleshat]] [[Category:Planetary leaders]]
